Inside The Huddle

A focus on mental health in the workplace

Inside the Huddle: A Mental Health in the Workplace featured a powerful conversation on mental health in the workplace, including expert insights and real stories of lived experience. We explored topics like, compassion fatigue, burnout, resilience, and personal stress management in hopes for people to learn practical strategies for dealing with them when they arise in the workplace. The event included two panel discussions, along with opportunities for networking and resource sharing before and after the main program.

Moderator:
Danny Waddle, Director of Behavioral Health, Ascension St. Vincent Stress Center

Panelists:
Dr. Dai Grose, DSW, LCSW, LCAC
Heather Baumgardner, LCSW
Carol Finkins
Alicia Hoover

experience depression, anxiety, or another mental health condition during pregnancy or in the year after birth.

2025 Inside the Huddle a focus on maternal mental health

Attendees had the opportunity to hear from experts and those with lived experiences discussing fertility and perinatal impacts on mental health, in addition to signs and symptoms to look out for postpartum.

ABOUT INSIDE THE HUDDLE

Inside the Huddle is an annual event partnership between Kicking The Stigma and Ascension St. Vincent during May’s Mental Health Awareness Month. Each year we will take a deep dive into a mental health topic with experts in the field.
